description_long: This object is a stylized cluster of orange flowers with six prominent petals each, radiating from a central point. The flowers are supported by long, slender, dark green stems that arch outward, giving the arrangement a fan-like appearance. Several narrow, lanceolate leaves emerge from the base, adding to the naturalistic look. The petals are arranged in a radial symmetry, with some flowers appearing more open and others partially closed or wilted. The coloration is primarily a warm orange for the petals and a deep green for the stems and leaves. The overall form is airy and delicate, resembling ornamental flowers often found in urban parks or landscaped sidewalks.
description: A cluster of orange, six-petaled flowers with long, arching green stems and narrow leaves, arranged in a natural, fan-like display.
description_view_0: Front view showing two fully open orange flowers and several arching stems with smaller blooms and narrow green leaves.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curvature of the stems and the layered arrangement of the flowers, with one flower more prominently facing forward.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the central flower and the spread of the stems, with leaves and smaller blooms visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the arching stems and the radial symmetry of the flower arrangement.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration, with each flower displaying six evenly spaced petals radiating from a central point. The arrangement is symmetrical and whorled, typical of many ornamental flowers.
